title: pneumonanthe montana
intro: Pneumonanthe montana, also known as lungwort, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its unique blue-green leaves with spots that resemble lungs. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to support respiratory health and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion and coughs. Pneumonanthe montana has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, and its health benefits also include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and its leaves can also be dried and used as a tea or added to herbal supplements.
